content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jan McMillan 
committee to Elect John Gillies 
1077 Klish Way 
Del Mar, 
CA 
92014 
Dear Ms. McMillan: 
March 27, 1986 
Re: 
FPPC Advice No. I-86-059 
This is in reply to your letter dated February 13, 1986, 
concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
This letter provides advice pursuant to the prov1s10ns of 
the Political Reform Act, and does not address the questions as 
they may be affected by the city of Del Mar's campaign ordinance. 
To determine whether there are any provisions in the City of Del 
Mar's campaign ordinance which may apply to the questions you ask, 
you must request an interpretation from the City of Del Mar. 
You asked if the Committee to Elect John Gillies 
("Committee") can buy or accept as an in-kind contribution any or 
all of the data acquired through a survey conducted by another 
committee and, if so, what the Committee's reporting requirements 
would be. 
Under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, if the 
Committee buys the survey results from the other committee at 
"fair market value," the Committee would report the payment to the 
other committee on Schedule E of the Form 490. 
The "fair market 
value" is whatever it would cost the Committee to purchase the 
information on the open market. 
This can be determined and 
provided to you by companies wnich are in the business of 
conducting such surveys, or it may be determined by using the 
actual costs which were incurred by the committee which conducted 
the survey. 
If the Committee accepts the survey results as an in-kind 
contribution from the other committee, or pays an amount which is 
less than the fair market value, the Committee must report the 
fair market value (or the difference between what it paid and the 
fair market value) as an "in-kind contribution" from the other 
committee on Schedule C of the Form 490. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Ms. Jan McMillan 
March 27, 1986 
Page 2 
I hope this clarifies the Committee's reporting requirements 
with regard to the survey results. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions. 
cc: 
Helen Arriola 
FPPC -
Los Angeles 
9=~ 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief, Technical Assistance 
& Analysis 




California 
,. 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jan McMillan 
Campaign Coordinator 
February 26, 1986 
Committee to Elect John Gillies 
1077 Klish Way 
Del Mar, CA 
92014 
Re: 
A-86-059 
Dear Ms. McMillan: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
J

28 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

sections.question: You asked if the Committee to Elect John Gillies ("Committee") can buy or accept as an in-kind contribution any or all of the data acquired through a survey conducted by another committee and, if so, what the Committee's reporting requirements would be.
sections.conclusion: Under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, if the Committee buys the survey results from the other committee at "fair market value," the Committee would report the payment to the other committee on Schedule E of the Form 490. The "fair market value" is whatever it would cost the Committee to purchase the information on the open market. If the Committee accepts the survey results as an in-kind contribution from the other committee, or pays an amount which is less than the fair market value, the Committee must report the fair market value (or the difference between what it paid and the fair market value) as an "in-kind contribution" from the other committee on Schedule C of the Form 490.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a campaign committee purchase or accept survey data conducted by another committee as an in-kind contribution, and what are the reporting requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If purchased at fair market value, report as a payment on Schedule E of Form 490. If accepted as an in-kind contribution or purchased below fair market value, report the fair market value (or the difference) as an in-kind contribution on Schedule C of Form 490.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the acknowledgment letter (Feb 26, 1986) and the substantive advice letter (March 27, 1986). Both letters reference the same advice number I-86-059. Acknowledgment letter states expected response time of 21 working days. The substantive advice letter provides clear question and conclusion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked if the Committee to Elect John Gillies ("Committee") can buy or accept as an in-kind contribution any or all of the data acquired through a survey conducted by another committee and, if so, what the Committee's reporting requirements would be.

CONCLUSION: Under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, if the Committee buys the survey results from the other committee at "fair market value," the Committee would report the payment to the other committee on Schedule E of the Form 490. The "fair market value" is whatever it would cost the Committee to purchase the information on the open market. If the Committee accepts the survey results as an in-kind contribution from the other committee, or pays an amount which is less than the fair market value, the Committee must report the fair market value (or the difference between what it paid and the fair market value) as an "in-kind contribution" from the other committee on Schedule C of the Form 490.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding the Committee to Elect John Gillies' ability to purchase or accept survey data from another committee, with guidance on fair market value determination and appropriate reporting on Form 490 schedules.
